target.name: Mainspring Energy
target.founder: Shannon Miller, Adam Simpson, Matt Svrcek

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mainspring Energy is a Series E climate and energy company founded by Shannon Miller, Adam Simpson, and Matt Svrcek that has raised $400M+. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 21 peer companies, though same-stage comparables are sparse—only 2 adjacent-stage private firms and 1 public comparable (LNZA) meet sector criteria, falling short of the 3+ datapoint threshold for robust peer-median analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($400M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E-to-later-stage progression yields $560M. This method is standard for private companies at this maturity level where financing history provides directional guidance in the absence of revenue or EBITDA benchmarks.

The peer-median approach is not applicable: while the broader Climate & Energy universe is sizeable, insufficient same-tier or adjacent-stage peers exist to establish a reliable median. Drawing on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise. The triangulated range is therefore anchored to stage-progression alone: $476M (low), $560M (median), $644M (high). Key uncertainties include unit economics maturity, path to commercialization timelines, and capital efficiency relative to the sparse peer set. Deeper diligence into technology readiness and market adoption curves would be essential before final valuation endorsement.
